---
title: "Evidence States and Claim Discipline"
chapter_id: "evidence-states-and-claim-discipline"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "spinoza"
  - "viea"
  - "uat"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Evidence States and Claim Discipline

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `evidence-states-and-claim-discipline` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `benchmaxxing`, `spinoza`, `viea`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `verification_bandwidth` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The living book needs a shared language for when a claim is only argued, source-derived, prototype-backed, or test-backed.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Without explicit support states, conceptual architecture prose can accidentally read like empirical proof.

## Core claim

[evidence-states-and-claim-discipline.core, support: argument] Every major claim should carry a support state and move only when source ingestion, prototype inspection, or actual tests justify the transition.

## Mechanism

- Use Appendix C as the claim ledger.
- Require source notes before promoting claims to source-derived.
- Require executed tests before test-backed labels.

## Interfaces

- Drafting updates claims.
- Experiments update evidence.
- Changelog records evidence movement.

## Invariants

- No fabricated source support.
- No fabricated test results.
- Negative and inconclusive results remain visible.

## Failure modes

- Support-state inflation.
- Citation laundering.
- Silent removal of failed claims.

## Minimal implementation

A claim record schema, support-state transition table, and validation check.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Support-state transition test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Claim ledger completeness test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Changelog consistency audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `uat` | Unified Adaptive Tribunal | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Multi-AI collaborative refinement/evaluation protocol. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
